summary:
Congenital syphilis and rickets often show peg-shaped incisors tapersing lingually; while some forms of Ectodermal Dysplasias may have associated tooth abnormalities, they do so less frequently without specific comorbidities like malnutrition. Therefore, peg-shaped incisors are least likely related to Supernumerary Teeth alone.
To summarize Agent1’s reasoning:

Peg-shaped incisors tapering toward the tongue tip are common in congenital syphilis and rickets but rare for isolated cases of supernumerary teeth or most types of ectodermal dysplasia absent significant co-morbidity. Hence, these abnormally shaped incisors would be unusual findings specifically linked only to supernumerary teeth rather than any listed condition including them directly. 

This summary captures two core aspects from the detailed analysis provided by focusing on prevalence across given options and highlighting why one stands out uniquely regarding this particular finding.
